title: textRender adds printable-range bound (ch 0x21..0x7e) that retail ASM lacks; retail renders control/DEL bytes via out-of-range font-table indexing
repro: render a text string containing a byte in 0x01..0x1f (excluding 0x0A) or 0x7f; port skips it (prev_char reset to 72, ptr advanced), retail emits a fontchar from font-table offset ch*24-0x318

evidence.path: src/game/textrelated.c:2176 retail ASM only gates ch<0x80 (slti 0x80) before construct_fontchar_microcode; port C textrelated.c:2014 adds ch>=0x21 && ch<=0x7e, and 2032 skips ch<0x80 control bytes

history.evidence: src/game/textrelated.c:2176 retail ASM only gates ch<0x80 (slti 0x80) before construct_fontchar_microcode; port C textrelated.c:2014 adds ch>=0x21 && ch<=0x7e, and 2032 skips ch<0x80 control bytes
history.note: After handling ch==0x20 (space) and ch==0x0A (newline), the retail US ASM at textrelated.c:2176 computes slti at,v0,0x80 and beqz at -> JP path, else falls straight into the ASCII construct_fontchar_microcode call using second_font_table + ch*24 - 0x318 (and prev_char*24 - 0x318). There is no lower/upper bound: every ch in 0x01..0x7f except 0x20 and 0x0A is rendered. For ch<0x21 the offset ch*24-0x318 is negative (reads before the table); for ch==0x7f it is one entry past the printable 0x21..0x7e range. The live C at textrelated.c:2014 instead guards ch>=0x21 && ch<=0x7e for the render, and textrelated.c:2032 routes any other ch<0x80 to prev_char=72; ptr++ (no render, no OOB read). Behavior-neutral for all valid printable ASCII (0x21..0x7e) and the JP path (ch>=0x80) which both match retail exactly; differs only for control/DEL bytes that retail renders via out-of-range font indexing. The added bound is present in BOTH the NATIVE_PORT and retail-form C branches (not NATIVE_PORT-gated). Rest of textRender verified equivalent: arga default = *(sft+0x88c)+*(sft+0x890), j_text_trigger && arga<14 -> 14, FA000000 header with arg6 identity byte-reassembly, space (*x+=5), newline (overlap_correction>=0 && *x==initial_x ? *y+=overlap : *y+=arga; *x=initial_x), full JP branch (24-byte D_80040EFC template copy, jis_code=((ch&0x7f)<<7|ptr[1]&0x7f)&0xffff, &0x2000 sets width15/height16, (jis&0x1fff)>=0x3c7 -> jis=2, index=jis+0x80, pixeldata=langGetJpnCharPixels), and all construct_fontchar_microcode arg tuples.

Bounded, content-unreachable renderer approximation (APPROXIMATIONS.md T1). Retail US textRender indexes second_font_table at ch*24 minus 0x318, equal to entry ch minus 33; for control bytes 0x01 to 0x1f excluding newline 0x0A the entry index is negative (reads before the table) and for DEL 0x7f it is entry 94, one past the printable 0x21 to 0x7e range. On the port that same indexing at textrelated.c 2018 is an out-of-bounds host array read (undefined behavior, never the N64 adjacent-memory bytes), so replicating retail is memory-unsafe. Byte-identical to retail for all printable ASCII 0x21 to 0x7e, space, newline, and the full JP path ch>=0x80; differs only for control and DEL bytes no authored game string is known to contain. Port narrows the render to 0x21 to 0x7e (textrelated.c 2014) and skips other ch<0x80 (textrelated.c 2032). Faithful-mode default is the narrowed bound.
